hypothesis: Increasing the branching density and cross-linking of amine-based polymer sorbents to form a hydrophilic, porous network will yield a non-linear increase in ultradilute CO2 uptake (0.4 mbar, 298 K) relative to linear polymers, and this improvement can be predicted and rapidly identified using a topology-aware polymer graph representation embedded in a multi-fidelity Bayesian optimization workflow.
